 Uragoga is a genus of flowering plants in the family Apocynaceae, characterized by its unique species that are known for their medicinal properties. The health benefits of Uragoga plants include their ability to treat fever, rheumatism, and other ailments, due to their bioactive constituents such as alkaloids, glycosides, and flavonoids. The bioactive constituents of Uragoga plants include alkaloids like voacangine and voacristine, which are responsible for their medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Uragoga include decoctions, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat various health conditions.
